title: Container class + scroll-shrinking header
task: Extract the page width wrapper into a reusable `container` class applied across the page, and make the header shrink in width to the container size on scroll.

stories.title: Introduce a scoped `container` class and use it page-wide
acceptanceCriteria: globals.css defines a `.container` rule scoped under `.page-shell` so it does NOT override Tailwind/fumadocs `container` utility used in docs layout
The `.container` rule sets width:100%, max-width:72rem (== max-w-6xl), margin-inline:auto, padding-inline:1rem with 1.5rem at >=640px (== px-4 sm:px-6)
PageShell root element carries the `page-shell` class
config/landing.ts SECTION constant equals "container" so every section/header/footer consumes the class
No section visually changes width (still centered at 72rem with the same horizontal padding)

stories.title: Header shrinks to container width on scroll
acceptanceCriteria: At scroll position 0 the header background+border spans the full viewport width with content aligned to the container
After scrolling (> ~8px) the visible header bar shrinks to the container width (72rem), centered, as a floating bar with border, shadow, rounding and a small top gap
The transition between the two states is animated (CSS transition) and reversible when scrolling back to top
Scroll state is tracked with a passive scroll listener that is cleaned up on unmount; no hydration mismatch
Works on both home and /sponsors pages and in light + dark themes
